event.summary: Claimed one ready iteration unit without expanding repository or path scope.

acceptance.proof: StreamInfoStaticFields borrows one accepted StreamDefinition, retains pointer identity for every borrowed text and metadata value, allocates nothing, exposes no mutable access, and leaves the definition reusable and unchanged. Consuming or cloning the source is not required.

The fixed descriptor-owned role order is name, type, channel_count, channel_format, source_id, nominal_srate. Original absent, present-empty, and present-nonempty content-type and source-id states remain observable, while separate effective accessors map only absence to the explicit empty string without trimming, normalization, case folding, or allocation.

All seven ChannelFormat variants map exactly and totally to the observed static symbols. NominalSampleRate remains available in its original Irregular or RegularHz form; the separate effective numeric view maps only Irregular to positive 0.0 and otherwise preserves the regular f64 bits. No decimal string formatting, clock read, cadence derivation, scheduling, or validation is added.

A new local-results overlay binds the accepted LSLC-001H case and observation artifacts and records candidate semantic-field results for exactly the seven accepted cases. It does not edit or reinterpret the oracle overlay, public normalized XML, capture hashes, runtime placeholders, description hierarchy, or full-document candidate_result.

The crate remains one dependency-free, feature-free, unsafe-free, source-only facade; Cargo files, feature and project locks, effects, XML serialization, runtime surfaces, and external authority boundaries remain unchanged. No device, package, network, oracle, FFI, adapter, provider, or Manifold behavior is introduced.

Public instructions route the static semantic projection and its strict limits; installed shared skills are reviewed for instruction impact; the portable workspace remains fail-closed with no device requirement.

document.commit_policy: Commit the borrowed static stream-info semantic projection separately from XML representation, numeric lexical formatting, description mapping, runtime-field models, complete document assembly, protocol, transport, adapters, providers, features, or external integration.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the six-role borrowed static-field projection, explicit option/rate views, observation binding, and strict no-XML/no-runtime/no-authority boundary.
Describe the first stream-info semantic adapter, its exact static mappings, source preservation, and the remaining XML, runtime, transport, and authority work.
Review whether the explicit static-versus-runtime field authority split adds reusable adapter or parameter-authority guidance; retain the skill unchanged if current rules already cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review whether the clean Rusty LSL static-field adapter needs new repo-family routing; retain the skill unchanged if current source, public-boundary, and Manifold-authority guidance already covers it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: Any change to Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, morphospace/feature.lock.json, morphospace/project.spec.json, existing accepted fixtures, oracle drivers, raw captures, LSLC-001H observations or provenance, external packages, dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, generated protocol material, or native libraries.

XML character validation, escaping or representation, element-name creation, element-tree construction, desc-root interpretation, serialization, declaration or whitespace policy, complete info documents, endpoint bytes, parsing, decoding, round trips, canonicalization, or mutation.

Formatting channel counts or nominal sample rates as strings, choosing floating-point precision or exponent policy, version fields, created_at, uid, session_id, hostname, IPv4 or IPv6 addresses, data or service ports, and any other runtime-populated or transport-owned field.

Oracle execution, network access, source-repository implementation inspection, or use of liblsl, rLSL, pylsl, wrapper, application, build, vendored, generated, protocol, or test source as implementation input. Only accepted public repository contracts and black-box observation artifacts may inform the local candidate mapping.

Discovery, resolution, inlet, outlet, samples, chunks, buffering, queues, clocks, correction, recovery, protocol, wire, sockets, networking, runtime activation, FFI, adapters, providers, devices, Quest, Hostess, or platform lifecycle.

Manifold stream admission, registry, subscription, route, lease, revision, replay, identity, authorization, audit, command, session, topology, transport, or product-policy authority, and commands derived from samples.
document.objective: Implement one dependency-free, borrowed, allocation-free semantic projection from an accepted StreamDefinition into the six descriptor-owned static stream-info fields, preserving original optional and nominal-rate forms while exposing explicit effective empty-option and irregular-rate values plus the seven observed channel-format spellings, without creating XML, assigning desc meaning, inventing runtime fields, or adding protocol, wire, I/O, transport, adapter, provider, device, or authority behavior.
outputs: A private stream_info_static_fields module exporting a public StreamInfoStaticFieldRole order and a public StreamInfoStaticFields borrowed view over one accepted StreamDefinition, with no owned allocation and no mutation or consumption of the source.

Accessors that preserve the original Option<&str> content-type and source-id forms while separately exposing the explicit effective empty-string values, preserve the original NominalSampleRate form and regular-Hz bits while separately exposing irregular as effective 0.0, and retain borrowed access to the unchanged extended MetadataTree without assigning it desc meaning.

An exact total mapping from all seven ChannelFormat variants to the observed static spellings float32, double64, string, int32, int16, int8, and int64, plus deterministic tests for the fixed six-role order, optional-state distinctions, pointer identity, all formats, irregular and regular rate semantics, and source preservation.

A separate LSLC-001K local-results overlay and focused checker covering the seven accepted LSLC-001H input cases at semantic-field level while leaving the LSLC-001H oracle overlay, normalized XML, runtime placeholders, and full-document candidate result unchanged.
